Claim_Text: Reward-trained low-dimensional motif selection improves held-out ORBIT user accuracy over the best fixed motif under paired inputs and budgets
Evidence_Source: EXP33 official ORBIT CLU-VE; paired user-level bootstrap and sign/permutation test
Notes: Primary endpoint is frame accuracy averaged within user before inference

Claim_Text: Benefits are mechanism-specific rather than generic ensembling
Evidence_Source: EXP33 actuator disagreement; quality/shot/occlusion strata; clamp delay and action-shuffle interventions
Notes: Requires directionally specific effects and Holm correction

Claim_Text: A high-rank E/I carrier retains at least 90% of controller gain with closed low-dimensional dynamics and normal contraction
Evidence_Source: EXP34 participating-carrier comparison; closure and perturbation audit
Notes: Cannot be supported by matrix rank alone

Claim_Text: The method improves very-low-shot accuracy versus personalization compute relative to gradient adaptation
Evidence_Source: ORBIT 1/2/4/8/16-shot sweep; MACs wall time and mutable-parameter audit; LN-Tune/linear-head baselines
Notes: Same frozen backbone and support tape

Claim_Text: ORBIT is a real-world personalized few-shot video benchmark with causal test constraints
Evidence_Source: Massiceti et al. ICCV 2021 plus official ORBIT protocol
Notes: Official source and code snapshot recorded in provenance

Claim_Text: Reusable dynamical motifs and gated fast-weight memories are plausible building blocks but do not establish actuator selection
Evidence_Source: Driscoll et al. 2024; Gated DeltaNet 2024/2026; Mamba-3 2026
Notes: Positioning only, not empirical support for C0
